meta.paper: The Irreducible Boundary of a Bounded Resolvable Space
meta.suite: concrete-space numerics, all 7 theorem-groups, randomized sweep

results.name: negation_uniqueness
results.claim: A = Omega\N(A); complement is an involution and a selector-free bijection between parts and negation-sequences.

results.name: non_instantaneity_residue
results.claim: k=0 individuates nothing (A_0=Omega); every proper individuation leaves an uncommitted separator of measure >= mu_min.

results.name: boundary_thickness
results.claim: beta_Part(A) >= mu_min > 0 for every realisable region; no sharp separator exists.

results.name: floor_agreement
results.claim: geometric, representational, and cost floors are all >= mu_min > 0 and bound the same constant; cost diverges as t->0.

results.name: detectability_sorites
results.claim: A distinguishable iff sigma(A) > beta(A); sub-floor regions engulfed; sorites aggregate emerges across a band with no single decisive grain.

results.name: diagonal_residue
results.claim: No total self-applicable verifier exists (Boolean fixpoint d=1-d unsolvable); hence the irreducible residue is not exhibitable as a positive-measure set.

results.name: non_return_cessation
results.claim: Committed record M is monotone non-decreasing (undo increments M); same-config different-record states are distinct; diverging cost vs bounded return forces a halt at t_* > 0.
